description: The Irish mob is a collection of organized crime groups composed of largely ethnic Irish members operating primarily in the United States. Originating in Irish-American street gangs in the early 19th century, it has appeared in most major U.S. and Canadian cities, especially in the Northeast and Midwest. The mob has been involved in various criminal activities including bootlegging, extortion, and drug trafficking. Notable groups include the Westies in New York.

country_links.evidence_quote: The West End Gang is one of Canada's most influential organized crime groups. Active since the early 1900s and still active today...
country_links.context: The Irish mob's West End Gang operates in Montreal, Canada, engaging in drug trafficking and other crimes.

country_links.evidence_quote: The ring imported liquor from Canada to Cedar Point and Toledo and then distributed the alcohol to the Chicago area.
country_links.context: The Irish mob operates in multiple U.S. cities including Cleveland, Detroit, and others, engaging in bootlegging, gambling, and drug trafficking.
